programmes.show_description: 5/5. Fear of never waking up turns to fear of waking up,  as Carl starts to stir. Concluded by Tom Goodman-Hill.
6/7. As the mud encroaches, Redlow is declared a Disaster Area and a complete evacuation is underway. Stars Maurice Denham.
4 Extra Debut. From Bach to Gloria Gaynor. Oscar-winning costume designer Jenny Beavan shares her music choices with Lauren Laverne. From 2023.
The famous naturalist looks at why underground animals evolved in a very different way from those on the surface. From 2009.
Daljit Nagra selects The Psalms read by actor Jeremy Irons featuring some of the most beautiful poetry in the Bible.  From 2019.
...Mole, Aged 13 3/4 (Omnibus). 1/2. It's 1981 and the year starts inauspiciously for Adrian Mole. A new girl called Pandora joins his class. Read by Harry McEntire.
Actor Mark Heap chooses 'Were You There?' by Tennessee Ernie Ford and 'L'Ascension' composed by Olivier Messiaen.
And in a packed programme, a future comedy star is born opposite a fire station. Ronnie Corbett reads from his autobiography.
David Barlow, Peter Christie, Miles Kington and Alan Maryon-Davis entertain with songs and sketches. From April 1985.
The King needs to entertain the peasants. So how about a lecture on ecology? Stars Jimmy Edwards and Joan Sanderson. From 1979.
1/5. The detective superintendent probes a grisly murder on a railway track. Nick Fisher's thriller stars Imelda Staunton.
1/5. A German and Englishman meet in East Africa in 1914.  Their striking resemblance gives the German an idea.  Stars David Robb.
2/4. In singles bars, Dr Heaven continues his search for love. And Frank. Though not simultaneously. Stars Richard Bean. From 1995.
15/20. When Mainwaring's bank gets bombed, the platoon must guard all the money. Adapted from BBC TV. With Arthur Lowe. From 1975.
3/4. Dr Johnson's celebrated biographer meets Harold Pinter, but becomes the victim of a betrayal. Stars Harry Enfield. From 2015.
6/10. Class Four D go on a school trip to the British Museum. Adrian has his tonsils out. Read by Harry McEntire.
1/6. 4 Extra Debut. There's a steady stream of customers at Bonnie's café where Bonnie knows everyone. Stars Prunella Scales and Hannah Gordon.
3/6. Phil Wang and Susie Dent challenge guests to invent new words and help create the new ultimate Unspeakable dictionary. With guests Ivo Graham, Harriet Kemsley and Nish Kumar.
4 Extra Debut. Max Cotton tries to complete a year of food self-sufficiency and explores ideas of eating seasonally and locally. From 2024.
